title: duranta plumieri var. normalis
intro: Duranta plumieri var. normalis, also known as the Blue Dew or Gold Mound Duranta, is a shrub-like plant that belongs to the Verbena family. It is native to the Caribbean and Central America, and is prized for its attractive blue flowers and golden foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and is said to have a range of health benefits, including reducing fever, treating wounds, and relieving respiratory issues. Duranta plumieri var. normalis can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and has been used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
